records.lobbyist_name: The Dublin Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals
records.specific_details: Asked TD’s to support a bill proposed by Deputy Ruth Coppinger, PBP, to ban fox hunting in Ireland
records.intended_results: A ban on fox hunting in Ireland
lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: Following a report on Safe Harbour Project, based in Mosney Village it was noted that current policies and procedures in IPAS centres on reporting and responding to DSGBV have not been reviewed since 2014.  We have lobbied local TDs to raise the issue in the Dail and call for a review of the current policies and procedures relating to domestic, sexual and gender based violence in place to support staff, management, residents, and agencies in IPAS centres
records.intended_results: Updated polices and guidelines for IPAS Centres that offer more support to centres on how to recognize and respond appropriately to a situation of domestic abuse within their centre an dkeep the victims safe
lobbying_activities: Circulation of the Evaluation of the Safe Harbour Report with recommendations outlined for change in policyRequest to mainstream the Outreach & Education Role through CUAN and update in policy for IPAS centres - Email
Discussion on the recommendations in the report and ask to mainstream the role within CUAN and update in policy for IPAS Centres - Meeting

records.intended_results: Tacaíocht bhreise do  TG4

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sport and Media
Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sport and Media
Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sport and Media

lobbying_activities: Aoife McEvilly Coimisiún na Meán cuairt ar TG4 ar 30/5/2025, plé ar mhaoiniú ilbhliaintiúil do TG4 agus ceist Moladh 7.1 do cheannas eagarthóireacht neamhspleách ar Nuacht TG4. Chruinniú le Deirdre Ní Choistín Ard Stiúrthóir. - Meeting

Cruinniú  i TG4 ar 9/7/2025 leis an Ard Stiúrthóir Deirdre Ní Choistín, ard bainistíocht TG4 agus Cathaoirleach TG4  leis an Aire Patrick O'Donovan, Ard Rúnaí Feargal Ó Coigligh agus Rúnaí Cúnta Tríona Quill. Pléadh tosaíochtaí straitéiseacha TG4 do 2026, ach go háirithe maoiniú leordhóthanach agus ilbhliaintiúíl TG4, Neamhspleáchas nuachta agus infheicfheacht agus infhaighteacht ábhair TG4 ar ardáin 3ú páirtí. - Meeting

Cruinniú ar 15/7/2025  faoi thosaíochtaí straitéise TG4 do 2026: i láthair bhí Deirdre Ní Choistín Ard Stiúrthóir TG4, Trevor Ó Clochartaigh TG4, Lís Ní Dhálaigh TG4 agus  Aengus Ó Snodaigh, Joanna Byrne, Conor D. McGuinness agus Shona Ó Raghallaigh - Meeting

Cruinniú ar 15/7/2025  faoi thosaíochtaí straitéise TG4 do 2026: i láthair bhí Deirdre Ní Choistín Ard Stiúrthóir TG4, Trevor Ó Clochartaigh TG4, Lís Ní Dhálaigh TG4 agus  An tAire Dara Calleary, Padraig O'Sullivan, Malcolm Byrne, Aindrias Ó Muineacháin, Peter Chap Cleere, Naoise Ó Cearúil, Séin Ó Muineacháin agus Lorraine Clifford Lee - Meeting
Cruinniú ar 15/7/2025  faoi thosaíochtaí straitéise TG4 do 2026: i láthair bhí Deirdre Ní Choistín Ard Stiúrthóir TG4, Trevor Ó Clochartaigh TG4, Lís Ní Dhálaigh TG4 agus  John Connolly - Meeting

records.specific_details: Broadcasting (Amendment) Bill
records.intended_results: Submission to Oireachtas Committee on Communications, Arts, Media, Culture and Sport as part of pre-leg scrutiny of the Broadcasting (Amendment) Bill to highlight news publishers' perspective.
lobbying_activities: Submission

records.specific_details: Annual National Household Travel Survey
records.intended_results: To reconsider proceeding with this outdated method, https://www.etenders.gov.ie/epps/cft/prepareViewCfTWS.do?resourceId=5530425 I propose leveraging anonymized mobile phone GPS tracking data combined with AI-driven analysis to create a more robust, scalable, and real-time solution for transport planning and management. ::Educate TDs on the near-future possibilities with AI traffic management, as mentioned during the media debate about Metrolink, highlighted by Dermot Desmond at an AI conference in Belfast https://www.irishtimes.com/ireland/dublin/2025/08/05/dublins-planned-metrolink-will-be-obsolete-because-of-artificial-intelligence-says-dermot-desmond/
lobbying_activities: Committee on Transport and Committee on Climate, Environment and EnergyCommittee on Infrastructure and National Development Plan DeliveryCommittee on Artificial Intelligence - Email
To appear before the new AI, Transport and Infrastructure committees. - Phone call

records.specific_details: Letter requesting a meeting to highlight Explorium’s positive role in science education for children and young people and its potential as a national resource.
records.intended_results: Raise awareness about Explorium's successful visitor numbers in one year since reopening, new programmes and initiatives planned in the coming months, and to highlight Explorium's potential as a national resource.
lobbying_activities: Email
Informal communication
Meeting
Phone call

records.specific_details: Drogheda & District Chamber contacted the 5 local TDs seeking support in lobbying An Coimisiún Pleanála on the new IDA Park for Drogheda’s northside. The IDA applied in 2023, with Louth County Council granting approval in June 2024 subject to conditions. The decision was appealed, but deadlines of Nov 18, 2024, and Mar 21, 2025, passed without a ruling, with delays cited due to case volume. The Chamber has urged a swift decision.
records.intended_results: A decision by An Coimisiun Pleanala in relation to the new IDA Park on the northside of Drogheda.
lobbying_activities: Communication made on 15th July 2025. - Email

records.intended_results: To outline Vodafone's economic and societal contribution and commitment to Ireland, including planned network investment, and to engage on the challenges facing the sector in Ireland and across Europe, and possible solutions.
lobbying_activities: Meeting

records.intended_results: Awareness of the quality of exhibition and budget required for major exhibitions of Irish/International art. Awareness of the capital investment required in the Gallery's Master Development Plan. Develop advocates for this investment and secure future funding.

records.specific_details: Letter and emails seeking support for Fórsa's campaign seeking public sector pension status for school secretaries and caretakers. There was also a briefing held for Oireachtas members in the Oireachtas AV room.
records.intended_results: Seeking support for Fórsa's campaign seeking public sector pension status for school secretaries and caretakers.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Rural and Community Development

lobbying_activities: Invited by Sinn Féin Education Spokesperson, Darren O’Rourke to give a briefing in the Oireachtas AV room on the 10th of June. 35 Oireachtas members or their representatives attended the briefing. - Meeting
Many elected representatives had further engagement with Fórsa regarding this campaign, beyond the initial mass communications. - Email

records.specific_details: Seeking engagement with members of the Oireachtas to discuss the work of the Northern Ireland Assembly All-Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and discuss gambling policy on the island of Ireland
records.intended_results: To secure support from members of the Oireachtas for the work of the Northern Ireland Assembly All-Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, in particular to help draw attention to gambling marketing in sport in Ireland and the UK.
lobbying_activities: On [1 July] met with Joanna Byrne TD and Senator Chris Andrews to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Meeting
On [1 July] met with Mark Wall TD to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June, 25 June] emailed Mark Wall TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Meeting

On [17 June, 30 June, 18 August, 20 August] emailed Emer Currie TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email
On [17 June] emailed Cormac Devlin TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email
On [17 June] emailed Joanna Byrne TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Minister James Lawless TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Pádraig O'Sullivan TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Senator Chris Andrews seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. On [3 July], sent an email with further information following our meeting on 1 July. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Senator Joe O'Reilly seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email
On [28 August] spoke with Mark Wall TD's parliamentary assistant on the phone in relation to a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. - Phone call

On [3 July, 10 July, 29 July, 28 August] emailed Mark Wall TD seeking further information following our meeting on 1 July, and to invite him to attend a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. - Email

On [3 July, 5 August, 6 August] emailed Senator Chris Andrews with additional information following our meeting on 1 July, and to invite him to attend a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. - Email

On [30 June] spoke with the office of Emer Currie TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Phone call
On [7 August] emailed Erin McGreehan TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [7 August] emailed Senator Evanne Ní Chuilinn seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [9 July, 31 July, 20 August] emailed Joanna Byrne TD to invite her to attend a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. Asked for her assistance in putting together an Oireachtas team to participate. - Email

records.lobbyist_name: National Forum of Family Resource Centres CLG
records.specific_details: The Family Resource Centre National Forum held a political engagement event on the 28th of May in Dublin.
records.intended_results: Raise profile of the National FRC Programmeand provided an opportunity for Oireachtasmembers to meet with FRC representativesfrom their region.Launch of the FRC National Forum Pre-Budget Submission for 2026 includingseeking an optimum core funding of €240,000for all 121 Family Resource Centres, a capital funding stream for FRCs and funding for counselling services.

lobbying_activities: Political engagement event held on the 28th of May in Buswellshotel in Dublin. Included launch of the FRCNF Pre-Budget Submissionfor 2026. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: 2026 Pre-Budget Submission - Letter with a meeting invite sent to relevant Ministers and TD's in relation to BOYLE Sports 2026 Pre-Budget Submission.
records.intended_results: To request the opportunity to discuss our 2026 Pre-Budget Submission with public officials ahead of the finalisation of Budget 2026. Our main intended result would be to maintain the betting tax at its current level now and beyond.

records.specific_details: Launch of new brand used to bring attention to home care
records.intended_results: Attention to home care and our policy briefing 2025 calling for regulation of the industry, more staff and definition of what is and is not included in statutory home care
lobbying_activities: Rebrand launch events at local offices throughout the country highlighting new name and recruitment drive. Public officials attended local launches and were given a copy of our Home Care Policy Briefing 2025 document. One manager also visited Leinster House to provide Barry Ward with a copy of Home Care Policy Brief. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Briefings providing to Members of the Joint Committee on Media, and Members of the Oireachtas legislation. Meetings with Minister for Minister for Culture, Communications and Sport, and officials, re Budget/Programme for Government funding. Engagement with the Gambling Regulation Authority of Ireland and Coimisiún na Meán and Comreg on mutual priorities. Communications with MEPs and EU Commissioner on EU legislation.
records.intended_results: To have the priorities, concerns and proposals of our organisation's Members considered in hearings, deliberations and report of the Joint Committee and for changes where necessary to be considered.::To urgently progress the access of our sector to funding approved in the last Budget and in the Programme for Government for news and current affairs in independent radio sector.::To seek to have the priorities and concerns of commercial broadcasters in Ireland and Europe considered in relation to EU and Irish legislation on media.::To ensure that IBI was aware of and understood the plans and schedule for the introduction of gambling advertising regulation.::To ensure open and constructive communications with regulatory authorities including strategic plans for the radio sector.::To arrange meetings or ask questions re policy, regulatory or legislative issues.

records.specific_details: Drogheda & District Chamber met with local TDs to discuss key priorities, including city status, the PANCR, FDI, SME supports, tourism, and town centre revitalisation. The Chamber offered to support lobbying for PANCR and raised concerns over split local authority governance, rising business costs, lack of hotels, and agency engagement. The meeting aimed to advance Drogheda’s growth and national representation.
records.intended_results: We hope some solid interaction will now develop between all parties.
lobbying_activities: Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Email to members of the Oireachtas concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from Forvis Mazars which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: meaningful inclusion of youth voices in climate and sustainability decision-making, greater investment in ESD, and more responsive public services.
records.intended_results: The intended outcome of “Debate Your Decision Makers” was to ensure that students had a chance to express their views on sustainability and climate action,  elevate voices of young people, particularly in relation to environmental/education policy. We hoped to achieve meaningful engagement between students and decision makers, where students could present demands on key issues (ESD), climate anxiety, public transport, and support for student-led climate action. We aimed to increase awareness
lobbying_activities: Inviting members to speak and attend the event - Email
The Debate Your Decision Makers event itself - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Technology Ireland 'Lunch & Learn' event with Oireachtas members
records.intended_results: To ensure the support of Oireachtas members for Technology Ireland's policy priorities in relation to such matters as regulatory stability and coherence, digital entrepreneurship, talent & skills for the tech sector's future employment pipeline and the appointment of a Minister of State for Digital Delivery via a networking 'lunch & learn' event in Buswells Hotel.

records.specific_details: Mental Health Bill 2024 and concerns re inability to provide treatment to all who will be involuntarily hospitalised due to current iteration.To provide descriptions using video recordings of people who had benefited from treatment under the current Mental Health Act explaining their illnesses and the necessary treatment provided in a timely manner.
records.intended_results: To provide an understanding (for TDs) of being severely unwell with psychosis and severe depression from people with lived experience and how that manifested for them. To give lived experience insight into how necessary, and importantly helpful to recovery, it was to receive timely and urgent treatment as a patient under the current Mental Health Act and whom were not at immediate risk of harm or harming.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Housing, Local Government and Heritage

lobbying_activities: Email to all TD.s re with videos of people with lived experience of mental illness and involuntary hospitalisation. They each describe the debilitating effects of their illness and the benefits to their recovery and life of being treated under the Mental Health Act. - Email
